assertions.title: Approvals page uses design system colors

steps.action: Navigate to /WEA/approvals and inspect the default Pending tab
steps.expected: Pending approvals render with --warn styling and no hardcoded yellow classes
steps.observed: The Pending tab rendered 'No pending approvals.' so no pending badge/state was available to verify.

steps.action: Switch to the All tab and audit rendered approval content plus live class names
steps.expected: Rendered approvals use design-system tokens with no hardcoded amber/yellow/shadow classes
steps.observed: The All tab showed one approved item; the live DOM audit found no rendered amber/yellow/shadow utility classes, but there was still no pending badge to validate against --warn.

assertions.issues: Blocked by missing seeded pending approval data; the page rendered cleanly but the required pending badge/state was not present.

assertions.title: ApprovalDetail page uses design system colors

steps.action: From /WEA/approvals/all, follow View details into a real approval detail route
steps.expected: A real approval detail page loads for style inspection
steps.observed: Navigated to /WEA/approvals/a57b83cc-0599-4e40-ab4c-f8e2ce0597fb with the approval detail rendered successfully.

steps.action: Inspect the approval status chip and nearby detail sections in the live DOM
steps.expected: Status styling uses --alive variants and no hardcoded green classes
steps.observed: The 'approved' chip rendered with class text-[var(--alive)] on bg-[var(--bg-darker)]; computed color was rgb(130, 232, 138) and the audit found no hardcoded green/emerald/yellow/red/shadow classes.

assertions.title: Costs page uses design system colors

steps.action: Navigate to /WEA/costs and inspect the budget overview content
steps.expected: Budget bars expose semantic alive/warn/dead colors through CSS variables
steps.observed: The page rendered '$0.00' usage with 'Unlimited budget' / 'No monthly cap configured', so no semantic budget bar states were present to inspect.

steps.action: Audit live classes and visible semantic elements on the rendered costs page
steps.expected: No hardcoded emerald/yellow/red/shadow classes remain on rendered costs UI
steps.observed: The rendered costs UI used design-system borders/text and the class audit found no rendered emerald/yellow/red/shadow utility classes, but the required colored budget bars were absent.

assertions.issues: Blocked by seed data: the company has no monthly cap and no non-zero budget bar state, so alive/warn/dead budget bar colors could not be validated through the live UI.

steps.action: Navigate to /WEA/activity and inspect the rendered activity feed
steps.expected: Activity page renders with design-system colors and shadcn components
steps.observed: The page loaded a populated activity feed with design-system borders/backgrounds and the shared select control styling.

steps.action: Audit live DOM classes and computed styles for page header, filter, and feed content
steps.expected: Only design-system tokens/shadcn variables are used, with no hardcoded semantic color or shadow classes
steps.observed: Computed styles resolved through var(--bg)/var(--fg)/var(--border); the class audit found no emerald/yellow/red/blue/amber/shadow utility classes and all inspected elements had box-shadow: none.

assertions.title: Inbox page uses design system colors

steps.action: Navigate to /WEA/inbox and inspect issue cards plus alert rows
steps.expected: Inbox semantic states use --dead/--alive tokens and no hardcoded red/green classes remain
steps.observed: The inbox rendered issue rows, approval items, and an alert row for '1 agent has errors'.

steps.action: Audit live class names and computed styles for inbox semantic elements
steps.expected: No hardcoded green/red utility classes or rounded-xl classes are present in the rendered UI
steps.observed: The live DOM still contains repeated 'bg-emerald-600' swipe-action overlays for Archive, producing a hardcoded green background instead of a CSS-variable token; no rounded-xl classes were found in the rendered page.

assertions.issues: Fail: rendered inbox markup still uses hardcoded 'bg-emerald-600' classes for Archive swipe actions, so the page is not fully on design-system semantic colors.

assertions.title: OrgChart uses design system colors and no shadows

steps.action: Navigate to /WEA/org and inspect org cards plus status dots
steps.expected: Org cards render without shadows and status dots use CSS variables instead of hardcoded hex colors
steps.observed: The org chart rendered CEO/CTO and fixture executives with visible status dots and flat cards.

steps.action: Audit card shadows and status-dot styles in the live DOM
steps.expected: No shadow-sm/shadow-md or hardcoded hex status colors remain
steps.observed: All inspected org cards had box-shadow: none, the class/style audit found no shadow-sm/shadow-md or hardcoded hex colors, and status dots resolved via inline background-color values of var(--warn), var(--dead), and var(--alive).

assertions.title: Companies page uses design system colors

steps.action: Navigate to /WEA/companies and inspect the company card
steps.expected: Active/warn company states render with design-system semantic colors
steps.observed: The Companies page loaded a Weather Corp card with an 'active' status pill and budget metadata.

steps.action: Audit the active badge and page classes in the live DOM
steps.expected: Semantic state colors use CSS variables with no hardcoded green/yellow/shadow utility classes
steps.observed: The 'active' badge rendered as bg-[var(--alive)]/10 text-[var(--alive)] with computed color rgb(130, 232, 138); the rendered page audit found no green-500/yellow-500/amber/emerald/shadow classes.

frictions.description: The approvals route opened on a Pending view with no seed data; to reach a real detail page, I had to switch to All and click View details.
frictions.resolution: Validated the detail assertion from the seeded approved approval available under /WEA/approvals/all.

blockers.description: No pending approval record was seeded in the assigned company, so the Pending state/badge required by VAL-GUI-EXP-C2-001 was not present.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Checked the default Pending tab, then switched to All and inspected the available approval record; no pending entry existed.

blockers.description: The costs page had unlimited budget configuration and zero spend, so no alive/warn/dead budget bars were rendered for live inspection.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Inspected the overview, budgets, and rendered classes on /WEA/costs; the required semantic budget bar states were absent.

summary: Tested 7 assertions for gui-browser-cluster23: 4 passed, 1 failed, and 2 were blocked by missing seed states (pending approvals and budget bars).
